entries.topic: (a) Definition of Economics according to Adams, Robbins, Keynes and Samuelson.
entries.source_excerpt: (a) Definition of Economics according to Adams, Robbins, Keynes and Samuelson. Self-explanatory.
(b) Micro & Macro Economics. Meaning, difference and examples only.
(c) Types of activities. Economic and non-economic activities: meaning and examples; difference between Economic and non- economic activities.

(d) Basic Economic terms. Meaning of the following economic terms: Wants, Goods, services, wealth, utility, production, consumption, sustainable consumption, factors of production, market, price, value, income, saving, wealth, welfare, economy. (e) Entities. Government sectors, firms, households, foreign sector: meaning only.
(a) On the basis of Nature. Capitalist Economy, Socialist Economy, Mixed Economy – with reference to India (meaning and differences).
(b) On the basis of Development. Developed Economy and Developing Economy: meaning and differences.
(c) Economic growth and development. Meaning and differences.

(a) Limited availability of Resources. Meaning and types of resources with examples; meaning and examples of limited resources; an understanding of the basic economic problem - Human wants are unlimited in relation to limited resources that have alternative uses; need for efficient use of resources (brief understanding with an example.)
(b) Central problems of an economy. What to produce? How to produce? For whom to produce? - A brief understanding with examples.

entries.topic: (i) Agriculture and allied activities: meaning; contribution of agriculture to employment, industry, trade
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Agriculture and allied activities: meaning; contribution of agriculture to employment, industry, trade and self-sufficiency in food production: a brief understanding of the above.

entries.topic: (ii) Problems of Indian Agriculture: a brief understanding of: limited use of technology, fragmentation
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Problems of Indian Agriculture: a brief understanding of: limited use of technology, fragmentation of land holdings, dependence on monsoon, lack of rural credit facilities, inadequate storage & marketing facilities.

entries.topic: (iii) Government measures to improve agricultural production: Use of High Yielding variety seeds, use of
entries.source_excerpt: (iii) Government measures to improve agricultural production: Use of High Yielding variety seeds, use of fertiliser and insecticides, Better irrigation facilities, Adoption of technology, Setting up of agricultural research centres and institutes, Provision & expansion of rural credit facilities. Provision of better storage and marketing facilities: a brief understanding of the above.

entries.topic: (iv) Green Revolution: meaning and its positive impacts.
entries.source_excerpt: (iv) Green Revolution: meaning and its positive impacts.

entries.topic: (v) Meaning of Food Security; role of Food Corporation of India (FCI).
entries.source_excerpt: (v) Meaning of Food Security; role of Food Corporation of India (FCI). (b) Secondary: Industry: meaning and types; adverse impacts of industrialisation and measures to overcome adverse impacts. (i) Meaning of Industry; a brief understanding of the interdependence of Agriculture and Industry. (ii) Types of Industries; large scale and medium scale Industries. Meaning, features and examples only. Cottage &Small-Scale Industries .Meaning, features and examples; significance with reference to India.

entries.chapter: Major Problems of the Indian Economy
entries.source_excerpt: (a) Poverty Meaning of poverty line- concept based on calorie intake; Relative and absolute poverty: meaning with examples; causes of poverty: any five causes of poverty to be discussed.

entries.chapter: Major Problems of the Indian Economy
entries.source_excerpt: (b) Unemployment Meaning; Types: Seasonal, Structural, Technological: meaning and example of each. Causes of unemployment- any five to be discussed.

entries.chapter: Major Problems of the Indian Economy
entries.topic: (c) Schemes and Programmes introduced by the government to remove poverty & unemployment.
entries.source_excerpt: (c) Schemes and Programmes introduced by the government to remove poverty & unemployment. Any two programmes to be studied with the objectives. For example, IRDP, JRY, PKVY, HRIDAY, MNREGA, etc.

entries.chapter: Major Reforms and Emerging trends in the Indian Economy
